title: Sorry Her Lot.
content:
Sorry her lot who loves too well,
    Heavy the heart that hopes but vainly,
    Had are the sighs that own the spell
    Uttered by eyes that speak too plainly;
    Heavy the sorrow that bows the head
    When Love is alive and Hope is dead!

    Sad is the hour when sets the Sun 
    Dark is the night to Earth's poor daughters
    When to the ark the wearied one
    Flies from the empty waste of waters!
    Heavy the sorrow that bows the head
    When Love is alive and Hope is dead!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Schwenck Gilbert, titled "Sorry Her Lot.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
